message:
feat(io): Excel roundtrip with index label preservation

crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs (+244):

New helpers:
 - `write_excel_index_label(sheet, row, col, label)`: serializes
   an index label (string, int, float, bool, or datetime) into an
   Excel cell in a format pandas' default reader will parse back
   to the same dtype.
 - `write_excel_scalar(sheet, row, col, scalar)`: typed scalar
   writer covering every DType variant so DataFrame cell values
   roundtrip correctly (in particular: Utf8 strings preserve
   leading zeros via inline string type, floats preserve NaN via
   missing-cell convention, booleans write as native Excel bools).

Roundtrip fixture tests:
 - `excel_default_read_exposes_written_index_as_first_column`:
   default writer reveals the index as the first column when
   read back with index_col=None, matching pandas default.
 - `excel_named_index_roundtrip_preserves_index_name`: named
   indexes retain their name across write+read and the first
   column header equals the index name.

Closes the IO round-trip parity gap for Excel named-index cases
that was blocking FP-P2D fixture promotion.
